title: Reduced motion and input preferences deterministic evidence bank

items.title: Reduced motion and input preferences: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling misconception? Reduced-motion users should receive no state feedback because every transition must be removed.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Preserve equivalent state feedback while removing or minimizing nonessential spatial and continuous motion.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Preserve equivalent state feedback while removing or minimizing nonessential spatial and continuous motion.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Preserve equivalent state feedback while removing or minimizing nonessential spatial and continuous motion.
Reduced-motion users should receive no state feedback because every transition must be removed.

answer.explanation: Preserve equivalent state feedback while removing or minimizing nonessential spatial and continuous motion.

items.title: Reduced motion and input preferences: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Respect reduced-motion and other user preferences with equivalent non-motion feedback. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating reduced-motion equivalent without movement; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

privateAuthorNotes: This code item and its oracle are AI-assisted draft material with reviewer=null and examEligibility=false.
Browser-verifier items are authoring evidence only and must never be routed to the official untrusted-code runner or formal exam pipeline.
items.starterCode: /* TODO: implement the declared bounded CSS facet. */

answer.referenceSolution: .action { transition: transform 180ms ease, background-color 180ms ease; } .action:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { scroll-behavior: auto !important; transition-duration: .01ms !important; animation-duration: .01ms !important; } .action:hover { transform: none; } }
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded css artifact for reduced-motion equivalent without movement. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
